visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/usa/en/nga/travel-insurance)
A transit visa is required if an applicant will have need to leave the airport, and therefore enter the country before completing a journey. Please provide:
- Passport and copy of data page(s) Passports should have a minimum of 6 months validity.
- Copy of online application form (SIGNED AND DATED)
- Payment and Acknowledgment Slips Printed
- 2 passport sized photographs
- Flight Itinerary (at least a copy of a flight booking, showing arrival and departure)
- Standard Processing Fee – All types of applications are $96.3 for non US citizens and $60 for US citizens Debit/Credit Card OR Money Order is accepted
MINORS (17 YEARS OLD AND UNDER)
- Passport and copy of data page(s) Passports should have a minimum of 6 months validity.
- Copy of online application form (SIGNED AND DATED)
- Payment and Acknowledgment Slips Printed
- 2 passport sized photographs
- Flight Itinerary (at least a copy of a flight booking, showing arrival and departure)
- $90.00 Debit/Credit Card -OR- Money Order (Standard Processing Fee – All types of applications)
- Copies of a letter of consent signed by both parents. You may download the form at Letter of Authorization for Minor (4 Copies)
- Copies of the child/children’s birth certificate. (4 Copies)
- Copies of both parents` valid passport data/signature pages. (4 Copies)
 Note:> Signatures and names should match those in their respective passports and names must match those on the birth certificate.   
In case where either parent has sole custody, a copy of a legal document should be provided to this effect, and in which case consent need only come from the single parent.
Click here to download PDF version of the checklist
visa_types.visa_fees:
Visa Fees for all visa categories is to be paid online while filling Visa application form on Nigerian Immigration portal.
All applicants need to pay a mandatory service fee of USD 96.3 if you are a Non US Citizen and USD 60 for US Citizen to be paid during submission at the visa application
                                                                                        center.
visa_types.processing_time:
The average processing time is 7 working days. In case the application is incomplete or queried and needs further investigation or in case the application has to be forwarded
                                                                                        to the competent authorities for further consideration, the assessment may take weeks or even longer. Visa applicants are therefore advised to submit their applications
                                                                                        approximately 4 weeks in advance.
visa_types.forms:
Kindly click here (https://portal.immigration.gov.ng/visa/freshVisa) to fill Online application form.
Please note that for submission of application at visa center it is important to carry printout of online application form duly filled online, payment acknowledgement
                                                                                        receipt of visa fees and copy of appointment letter schedule online.
Please click here to schedule an appointment to submit your application at Nigeria visa application center.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Business visa is for those applicants who wish to travel to Nigeria for the purpose of transiting Nigeria and then traveling to another country. 
A transit visa is required if an applicant will have need to leave the airport, and therefore enter the country before completing a journey.
Children under the age of five (5) and adults over the age of sixty-five (65)
																				are not required to be physically present at the Visa Submission Centers. Applicable documents can be mailed to the visa processing Center. 
145 W 45th Street
																				4th FL
																				New York, NY 10036
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Business visa is for those applicants who wish to travel to Nigeria for the purpose of transiting Nigeria and then traveling to another country. 
A transit visa is required if an applicant will have need to leave the airport, and therefore enter the country before completing a journey.
Children under the age of five (5) and adults over the age of sixty-five (65)
																				are not required to be physically present at the Visa Submission Centers. Applicable documents can be mailed to the visa processing Center. 
145 W 45th Street
																				4th FL
																				New York, NY 10036

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees for all visa categories is to be paid online while filling Visa application form on Nigerian Immigration portal.
All applicants need to pay a mandatory service fee of USD 96.3 if you are a Non US Citizen and USD 60 for US Citizen to be paid during submission at the visa application
                                                                                        center.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/usa/en/nga/travel-insurance)
A transit visa is required if an applicant will have need to leave the airport, and therefore enter the country before completing a journey. Please provide:
- Passport and copy of data page(s) Passports should have a minimum of 6 months validity.
- Copy of online application form (SIGNED AND DATED)
- Payment and Acknowledgment Slips Printed
- 2 passport sized photographs
- Flight Itinerary (at least a copy of a flight booking, showing arrival and departure)
- Standard Processing Fee – All types of applications are $96.3 for non US citizens and $60 for US citizens Debit/Credit Card OR Money Order is accepted
MINORS (17 YEARS OLD AND UNDER)
- Passport and copy of data page(s) Passports should have a minimum of 6 months validity.
- Copy of online application form (SIGNED AND DATED)
- Payment and Acknowledgment Slips Printed
- 2 passport sized photographs
- Flight Itinerary (at least a copy of a flight booking, showing arrival and departure)
- $90.00 Debit/Credit Card -OR- Money Order (Standard Processing Fee – All types of applications)
- Copies of a letter of consent signed by both parents. You may download the form at Letter of Authorization for Minor (4 Copies)
- Copies of the child/children’s birth certificate. (4 Copies)
- Copies of both parents` valid passport data/signature pages. (4 Copies)
 Note:> Signatures and names should match those in their respective passports and names must match those on the birth certificate.   
In case where either parent has sole custody, a copy of a legal document should be provided to this effect, and in which case consent need only come from the single parent.
Click here to download PDF version of the checklist

## Photo Specifications

## Photograph Quality
- The photographs must be:
- No more than 6-months old
- 35-40mm in width
- Close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70 -80% of the photograph
- In sharp focus and clear
- Of high quality with no ink marks or creases
- The photographs must:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show your skin tones naturally
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast
- Be printed on high quality paper, and at high resolution Photographs taken with a digital camera
                                                                                                    must be high quality colour and printed on photo-quality paper.

## Style and lighting
- The photographs must be:
- Be colour neutral
- Show your eyes open and clearly visible-no hair across your eyes
- Show you facing square on to the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted,
                                                                                                    and showing both edges of your face clearly
- Be taken with a plain light-coloured background
- Be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red
                                                                                                    eye

## Glasses and head covers
- If you wear glasses:
- Be colour neutral
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, and no tinted
                                                                                                    lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames - wear lighter framed glasses if you have them)
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Head coverings :
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to
                                                                                                    top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.

## Expression and frame
- Your photographs must:
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral
                                                                                                    expression and your mouth closed.

## Processing Time
The average processing time is 7 working days. In case the application is incomplete or queried and needs further investigation or in case the application has to be forwarded
                                                                                        to the competent authorities for further consideration, the assessment may take weeks or even longer. Visa applicants are therefore advised to submit their applications
                                                                                        approximately 4 weeks in advance.

## Download Forms
 Kindly click here (https://portal.immigration.gov.ng/visa/freshVisa) to fill Online application form.
Please note that for submission of application at visa center it is important to carry printout of online application form duly filled online, payment acknowledgement
                                                                                        receipt of visa fees and copy of appointment letter schedule online.
Please click here to schedule an appointment to submit your application at Nigeria visa application center.

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/usa/en/nga/travel-insurance)
- Passport and copy of data page(s) Passports should have a minimum of 6 months validity.
- Copy of online application form (SIGNED AND DATED)
- Payment and Acknowledgment Slips Printed
- 2 passport sized photographs
- Flight Itinerary (at least a copy of a flight booking, showing arrival and departure)
- Standard Processing Fee – All types of applications are $96.3 for non US citizens and $60 for US citizens Debit/Credit Card OR Money Order is accepted
- Bank Statement (showing adequate funds) / Letter of undertaking from employer
- Business invitation on business letterhead addressed to the Consulate General of Nigeria. It must be on letter headed paper stating the address of the host organization, the reason
                                                                            for the visit and maybe be signed and dated.
- Certificate of Incorporation (a copy provided by the business in Nigeria – government bodies, major banks and oil companies excluded)
- A letter of introduction from the Employer.
- Documentary evidence of need for multiple entry visa.
Note: Travelers on Religious or Journalistic visit fall under the business category BUT will not need to provide item no. 9 above. However, these applications can take
                                                                        up to six (6) weeks to process; applicants should therefore make provision for this and apply early to the relevant Nigerian authorities for clearance. 
Click here to download PDF version of the checklist
visa_types.visa_fees:
Visa Fees for all visa categories is to be paid online while filling Visa application form on Nigerian Immigration portal.
All applicants need to pay a mandatory service fee of USD 96.3 if you are a Non US Citizen and USD 60 for US Citizen to be paid during submission at the visa application center.
visa_types.processing_time:
The average processing time is 7 working days. In case the application is incomplete or queried and needs further investigation or in case the application has to be forwarded to the competent
                                                                        authorities for further consideration, the assessment may take weeks or even longer. Visa applicants are therefore advised to submit their applications approximately 4 weeks in advance.
visa_types.forms:
Kindly click here (https://portal.immigration.gov.ng/visa/freshVisa) to fill Online application form.
Please note that for submission of application at visa center it is important to carry printout of online application form duly filled online, payment acknowledgement receipt of visa
                                                                        fees and copy of appointment letter schedule online.
Please click here to schedule an appointment to submit your application at Nigeria visa application center.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Business visa is for those applicants who wish to travel to Nigeria for the purpose of Business conference and meetings, attending conferences and any other Business related meetings.
                                                                        
Note: Travelers on Religious or Journalistic visit fall under the business category BUT will not need to provide item no. 9 above. However, these applications can take
                                                                        up to six (6) weeks to process; applicants should therefore make provision for this and apply early to the relevant Nigerian authorities for clearance.
Children under the age of five (5) and adults over the age of sixty-five (65)
																				are not required to be physically present at the Visa Submission Centers. Applicable documents can be mailed to the visa processing Center. 
145 W 45th Street
																				4th FL
																				New York, NY 10036

## Overview
Business visa is for those applicants who wish to travel to Nigeria for the purpose of Business conference and meetings, attending conferences and any other Business related meetings.
                                                                        
Note: Travelers on Religious or Journalistic visit fall under the business category BUT will not need to provide item no. 9 above. However, these applications can take
                                                                        up to six (6) weeks to process; applicants should therefore make provision for this and apply early to the relevant Nigerian authorities for clearance.
Children under the age of five (5) and adults over the age of sixty-five (65)
																				are not required to be physically present at the Visa Submission Centers. Applicable documents can be mailed to the visa processing Center. 
145 W 45th Street
																				4th FL
																				New York, NY 10036

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees for all visa categories is to be paid online while filling Visa application form on Nigerian Immigration portal.
All applicants need to pay a mandatory service fee of USD 96.3 if you are a Non US Citizen and USD 60 for US Citizen to be paid during submission at the visa application center.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/usa/en/nga/travel-insurance)
- Passport and copy of data page(s) Passports should have a minimum of 6 months validity.
- Copy of online application form (SIGNED AND DATED)
- Payment and Acknowledgment Slips Printed
- 2 passport sized photographs
- Flight Itinerary (at least a copy of a flight booking, showing arrival and departure)
- Standard Processing Fee – All types of applications are $96.3 for non US citizens and $60 for US citizens Debit/Credit Card OR Money Order is accepted
- Bank Statement (showing adequate funds) / Letter of undertaking from employer
- Business invitation on business letterhead addressed to the Consulate General of Nigeria. It must be on letter headed paper stating the address of the host organization, the reason
                                                                            for the visit and maybe be signed and dated.
- Certificate of Incorporation (a copy provided by the business in Nigeria – government bodies, major banks and oil companies excluded)
- A letter of introduction from the Employer.
- Documentary evidence of need for multiple entry visa.
Note: Travelers on Religious or Journalistic visit fall under the business category BUT will not need to provide item no. 9 above. However, these applications can take
                                                                        up to six (6) weeks to process; applicants should therefore make provision for this and apply early to the relevant Nigerian authorities for clearance. 
Click here to download PDF version of the checklist

## Photo Specifications

## Photograph Quality
- The photographs must be:
- No more than 6-months old
- 35-40mm in width
- Close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70 -80% of the photograph
- In sharp focus and clear
- Of high quality with no ink marks or creases
- The photographs must:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show your skin tones naturally
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast
- Be printed on high quality paper, and at high resolution Photographs taken with a digital camera must be high quality
                                                                                    colour and printed on photo-quality paper.

## Style and lighting
- The photographs must be:
- Be colour neutral
- Show your eyes open and clearly visible-no hair across your eyes
- Show you facing square on to the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted, and showing
                                                                                    both edges of your face clearly
- Be taken with a plain light-coloured background
- Be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red eye

## Glasses and head covers
- If you wear glasses:
- Be colour neutral
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, and no tinted lenses (if possible,
                                                                                    avoid heavy frames - wear lighter framed glasses if you have them)
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Head coverings :
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead
                                                                                    and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.

## Expression and frame
- Your photographs must:
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression
                                                                                    and your mouth closed.

## Processing Time
The average processing time is 7 working days. In case the application is incomplete or queried and needs further investigation or in case the application has to be forwarded to the competent
                                                                        authorities for further consideration, the assessment may take weeks or even longer. Visa applicants are therefore advised to submit their applications approximately 4 weeks in advance.

## Download Forms
 Kindly click here (https://portal.immigration.gov.ng/visa/freshVisa) to fill Online application form.
Please note that for submission of application at visa center it is important to carry printout of online application form duly filled online, payment acknowledgement receipt of visa
                                                                        fees and copy of appointment letter schedule online.
Please click here to schedule an appointment to submit your application at Nigeria visa application center.

visa_types.name: Temporary Work Permit (TWP)
visa_types.documents_required:
TWP VISA REQUIRMENTS
- Passport and copy of data page(s) Passports should have a minimum of 6 months validity.
- Copy of online application form (SIGNED AND DATED)
- Payment and Acknowledgment Slips Printed
- 2 passport sized photographs
- Flight Itinerary (at least a copy of a flight booking, showing arrival and departure)
- Standard Processing Fee – All types of applications are $96.3 for non US citizens and $60 for US citizens Debit/Credit Card OR Money Order is accepted
- Letter of employment stating position
- Applicant’s letter of acceptance .
- A letter of introduction/invitation stating position from the host organization in Nigeria. This must be addressed to The Consulate General of Nigeria. It
                                                                                                    must be on letter headed paper stating the address of the host organization, the reason for the visit and must be signed and dated.
- Copy of Comptroller General of Immigration's approval cable visa that lists the applicant’s name.
Note: This document is obtained by the employer from the Nigeria Immigration Service in Nigeria on behalf of the employee; the applicant must
                                                                                                provide this document when submitting the visa application to the Consulate.
Click here to download PDF version of the Checklist
visa_types.visa_fees:
Visa Fees for all visa categories is to be paid online while filling Visa application form on Nigerian Immigration portal.
All applicants need to pay a mandatory service fee of USD 96.3 if you are a Non US Citizen and USD 60 for US Citizen to be paid during submission at the visa application
                                                                                                center.
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/usa/en/nga/travel-insurance)
visa_types.processing_time:
The average processing time is 7 working days. In case the application is incomplete or queried and needs further investigation or in case the application has
                                                                                                to be forwarded to the competent authorities for further consideration, the assessment may take weeks or even longer. Visa applicants are therefore advised
                                                                                                to submit their applications approximately 4 weeks in advance.

                                                                                                receipt of visa fees and copy of appointment letter schedule online.
Please click here to schedule an appointment to submit your application at Nigeria visa application center.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
TWP visa are for those applicants who wish to travel to Nigeria fir work for a temporary period. TWP Visas are issued with a maximum approved stay of ninety (90)
                                                                                                days from date of entry BUT are renewable in Nigeria (subject to approval). 
Children under the age of five (5) and adults over the age of sixty-five (65)
																				are not required to be physically present at the Visa Submission Centers. Applicable documents can be mailed to the visa processing Center. 
145 W 45th Street
																				4th FL
																				New York, NY 10036

 Kindly click here (https://portal.immigration.gov.ng/visa/freshVisa) to fill Online application form.

                                                                                                receipt of visa fees and copy of appointment letter schedule online.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/usa/en/nga/travel-insurance)
Note: Visitor/Tourist visas when issued are valid for a 90-day period of stay from date of entry into Nigeria.
Note: For all visa types listed below, the approved period of stay begins from date of entry, however, applicants will have a period of 90 days from issue to
                                                                                make use of the entry visa after which time, the visa becomes invalid and the applicant will have to re-apply. This includes multiple entry visas as per the first entry into
                                                                                the country.
- Passport and copy of data page(s) Passports should have a minimum of 6 months validity.
- Copy of online application form (SIGNED AND DATED)
- Payment and Acknowledgment Slips Printed
- 2 passport sized photographs
- Flight Itinerary (at least a copy of a flight booking, showing arrival and departure)
- Standard Processing Fee – All types of applications are $96.3 for non US citizens and $60 for US citizens Debit/Credit Card OR Money Order is accepted
- Bank Statement (showing adequate funds, minimum $1,000.00)
- A letter of invitation from the host addressed to the Consulate General of Nigeria. It must be on letter headed paper stating the address of the host or organization, the
                                                                                    reason for the visit and should be signed and dated.
- A copy of the host’s passport (the data pages including the photograph and signature, for non-Nigerians including the page that shows the host’s legal immigration status in
                                                                                    Nigeria
- Hotel Reservation
Note: A holder of an expired Nigerian passport with dual-nationality may submit a copy and will therefore NOT be required to submit items 8-10.
Click here to download PDF version of the Checklist
visa_types.visa_fees:
Visa Fees for all visa categories is to be paid online while filling Visa application form on Nigerian Immigration portal.
All applicants need to pay a mandatory service fee of USD 96.3 if you are a Non US Citizen and USD 60 for US Citizen to be paid during submission at the visa application center.
visa_types.processing_time:
The average processing time is 7 working days. In case the application is incomplete or queried and needs further investigation or in case the application has to be forwarded
                                                                                to the competent authorities for further consideration, the assessment may take weeks or even longer. Visa applicants are therefore advised to submit their applications approximately
                                                                                4 weeks in advance.
visa_types.forms:
Kindly click here (https://portal.immigration.gov.ng/visa/freshVisa) to fill Online application form.
Please note that for submission of application at visa center it is important to carry printout of online application form duly filled online, payment acknowledgement receipt
                                                                                of visa fees and copy of appointment letter schedule online.
Please click here to schedule an appointment to submit your application at Nigeria visa application center.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Tourist visa are for those applicants who wish to visit Nigeria for tourism purpose. 
Note: Visitor/Tourist visas when issued are valid for a 90-day period of stay from date of entry into Nigeria. 
Note: For all visa types listed below, the approved period of stay begins from date of entry, however, applicants will have a period of 90 days from issue to
                                                                                make use of the entry visa after which time, the visa becomes invalid and the applicant will have to re-apply. This includes multiple entry visas as per the first entry into
                                                                                the country. 
Children under the age of five (5) and adults over the age of sixty-five (65)
																				are not required to be physically present at the Visa Submission Centers. Applicable documents can be mailed to the visa processing Center. 
145 W 45th Street
																				4th FL
																				New York, NY 10036

Click here to download PDF version of the Checklist

## Photo Specifications

## Photograph Quality
- The photographs must be:
- No more than 6-months old
- 35-40mm in width
- Close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70 -80% of the photograph
- In sharp focus and clear
- Of high quality with no ink marks or creases
- The photographs must:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show your skin tones naturally
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast
- Be printed on high quality paper, and at high resolution Photographs taken with a digital camera must be
                                                                                            high quality colour and printed on photo-quality paper.

## Style and lighting
- The photographs must be:
- Be colour neutral
- Show your eyes open and clearly visible-no hair across your eyes
- Show you facing square on to the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted, and
                                                                                            showing both edges of your face clearly
- Be taken with a plain light-coloured background
- Be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red eye

## Glasses and head covers
- If you wear glasses:
- Be colour neutral
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, and no tinted lenses
                                                                                            (if possible, avoid heavy frames - wear lighter framed glasses if you have them)
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Head coverings :
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead
                                                                                            and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.

## Expression and frame
- Your photographs must:
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression
                                                                                            and your mouth closed.

## Processing Time
The average processing time is 7 working days. In case the application is incomplete or queried and needs further investigation or in case the application has to be forwarded
                                                                                to the competent authorities for further consideration, the assessment may take weeks or even longer. Visa applicants are therefore advised to submit their applications approximately
                                                                                4 weeks in advance.

## Download Forms
 Kindly click here (https://portal.immigration.gov.ng/visa/freshVisa) to fill Online application form.
Please note that for submission of application at visa center it is important to carry printout of online application form duly filled online, payment acknowledgement receipt
                                                                                of visa fees and copy of appointment letter schedule online.
Please click here to schedule an appointment to submit your application at Nigeria visa application center.

visa_types.name: Subject to Regularization (STR)
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/usa/en/nga/travel-insurance)
PRIMARY APPLICANT
- Passport and copy of data page(s) Passports should have a minimum of 6 months validity.
- Copy of online application form (SIGNED AND DATED)
- Payment and Acknowledgment Slips Printed
- 2 passport sized photographs
- Flight Itinerary (at least a copy of a flight booking, showing arrival and departure)
- Standard Processing Fee – All types of applications are $96.3 for non US citizens and $60 for US citizens Debit/Credit Card OR Money Order is accepted
- Copies of the child/children’s birth certificate. (4 Copies)
- Copies of both parents` valid passport data/signature pages. (4 Copies)
Note: Standard processing for STR Visa is fourteen (14) business days.
Documents below should be submitted in four (4) duplicate sets and arranged in the following order:
- Page 1 – Form (online form) with one (1) colored passport sized photograph (on white background) attached.
- Page 2 – Copy of letter of request from the company addressed to the Consulate General of Nigeria requesting STR visa for the applicant, this must contain the applicant’s full name.
- Page 3 – Copy of expatriate quota stating position of employment
- Page 4 – Copy of employment letter stating position employment
- Page 5 – Copy of applicant’s signed acceptance letter stating position of employment
- Page 6 – Copies of qualifications: degree, diploma certificates matching those mentioned on the resume.
- Page 7 – Copy of credentials, certificated and curriculum vitae, all vetted by a relevant official of Nigerian High Commission/Embassy (English Certified translated copy where applicable)
Note: Applicants will also need to submit originals of certificates for inspection. Please submit these Enclosed in a separate envelope marked ‘ORIGINAL CERTIFICATES’
STR (SPOUSE)
- Passport and copy of data page(s) Passports should have a minimum of 6 months validity.
- Copy of online application form (SIGNED AND DATED)
- Payment and Acknowledgment Slips Printed
- 2 passport sized photographs
- Flight Itinerary (at least a copy of a flight booking, showing arrival and departure)
- Standard Processing Fee – All types of applications are $96.3 for non US citizens and $60 for US citizens Debit/Credit Card OR Money Order is accepted
- Spouse’s Bank Statement (showing adequate funds FOR STRS’ A MINIMUM OF $500 IN CREDIT NO MORE THAN 3 MONTHS OLD AT TIME OF SUBMISSION).
Documents below should be submitted in four (4) duplicate sets + arranged in the following order:
- Page 1 – form (online form) with one (1) colored passport sized photograph (on white background) attached.
- Page 2 – Copy of letter of request from the company addressed to the Consulate General of Nigeria requesting STR visa for the applicant, this must
                                                                                                            contain the applicants full name.
- Page 3 – Copies of spouse’s expatriate quota stating position of employment.
- Page 4 – Copies of spouse’s employment letter stating position employment
- Page 5 – Copies of spouse’s signed acceptance letter stating position of employment
- Page 6 – Copies of marriage certificate
Note:Holders of Non-US passports MUST provide proof of legal residency (photocopy of green-card).
                                                                                                    
STR (CHILDREN)
- Passport and copy of data page(s) Passports should have a minimum of 6 months validity.
- Copy of online application form (SIGNED AND DATED)
- Payment and Acknowledgment Slips Printed
- 2 passport sized photographs
- Flight Itinerary (at least a copy of a flight booking, showing arrival and departure)
- Standard Processing Fee – All types of applications are $96.3 for non US citizens and $60 for US citizens Debit/Credit Card OR Money Order is accepted
- Copies of a letter of consent signed by both parents. You may download the form at Letter of Authorization for Minor (4 Copies).
- Copies of the child/children’s birth certificate (4 Copies).
- Copies of both parents` valid passport data/signature pages (4 Copies).
- Parents Bank Statement (showing adequate funds FOR STRS’ A MINIMUM OF $500 IN CREDIT NO MORE THAN 3 MONTHS OLD AT TIME OF SUBMISSION)
- Parents Bank Statement (showing adequate funds FOR STRS’ A MINIMUM OF $500 IN CREDIT NO MORE THAN 3 MONTHS OLD AT TIME OF SUBMISSION)
- 4 passport sized photographs
Documents listed below should be submitted in four (4) duplicate sets per child and arranged in the following order: :
                                                                                                    
- Page 1 – Form (online form) with one (1) colored passport (on white background) attached.
- Page 2 – Copy of letter of request from the company addressed to the Consulate General of Nigeria requesting STR visa for the applicant, this must
                                                                                                            contain the applicants full name.
- Page 3 – Copy of parent’s expatriate quota stating position of employment
- Page 4 – Copy of parent’s employment letter stating position of employment
- Page 5 – Copy of parent’s signed acceptance letter stating position of employment
- Page 6 – Copy of child’s birth certificate
Click here to download PDF version of the checklist

visa_types.overview:
Overview
STR visa are for those applicants who wish to travel to Nigeria for the purpose of employment. 
Note: STR Visas are issued with approved stay for a period of two (2) years BUT are renewable in Nigeria (subject to approval).
Children under the age of five (5) and adults over the age of sixty-five (65)
																				are not required to be physically present at the Visa Submission Centers. Applicable documents can be mailed to the visa processing Center. 
145 W 45th Street
																				4th FL
																				New York, NY 10036
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
STR visa are for those applicants who wish to travel to Nigeria for the purpose of employment. 
Note: STR Visas are issued with approved stay for a period of two (2) years BUT are renewable in Nigeria (subject to approval).
Children under the age of five (5) and adults over the age of sixty-five (65)
																				are not required to be physically present at the Visa Submission Centers. Applicable documents can be mailed to the visa processing Center. 
145 W 45th Street
																				4th FL
																				New York, NY 10036

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees for all visa categories is to be paid online while filling Visa application form on Nigerian Immigration portal.
All applicants need to pay a mandatory service fee of USD 96.3 if you are a Non US Citizen and USD 60 for US Citizen to be paid during submission at the
                                                                                                        visa application center.

- Copies of the child/children’s birth certificate. (4 Copies)
- Copies of both parents` valid passport data/signature pages. (4 Copies)
Note: Standard processing for STR Visa is fourteen (14) business days.

## Photograph Quality
